judgments.justification: Massive hallucination. Misidentifies the platform as 'Erno', the user as 'Frankie', and fabricates nearly all album titles (Voodoo, Winds, Galactic Cruise, etc.). Almost nothing matches the actual image.

notes: This image tests two distinct skills: reading clearly visible titles (15 albums) and reading titles only on small thumbnail covers in row 4 (5 albums: Secreto del Sur, SOL SOL, Ocean Flute, Spirits of the Wind, Timeless Whispers). The Qwen3.5/3.6 family dominated, with two perfect scores (qwen3.5-9b|nothink and qwen3.6-35b-a3b|nothink) and several near-perfect responses limited only by output truncation. The Gemma models struggled significantly: gemma4-12b|think produced catastrophic hallucinations (claiming the platform is 'Erno' with fake titles like 'Voodoo' and 'Galactic Cruise'), while gemma4-31b|think fabricated 'Sueños del Sol' and 'OCEAN OF LIGHT'. Notably, the smaller Qwen3-VL-4b outperformed all Gemma variants, suggesting Qwen's OCR/training is particularly strong on dense catalog screenshots. Truncation was a recurring issue across think-mode variants but never produced hallucinated content - the failure mode was always missing content rather than invented content. The |nothink variants of Qwen3.5/3.6 generally produced more complete (non-truncated) responses than their |think counterparts.
